scores.reasoning: This paper tackles a critical and under-explored safety issue in multilingual contexts with a seemingly thorough evaluation and a proposed defense mechanism.
The paper proposes a novel and intuitive plugin to improve existing SOTA models by exploiting lead-lag relationships, demonstrating significant empirical gains.
This work presents a well-motivated method for GNN compression that achieves impressive results on a large-scale dataset, making a practical contribution.
The paper introduces a novel and valuable method for analyzing and improving SFT data, directly leading to a more performant model.
This paper proposes an interesting, cognitively-inspired method for pose estimation that reportedly achieves strong results on standard benchmarks.
The paper presents a highly novel approach to retrieval that smartly leverages the generative power of LLMs, potentially simplifying complex RAG pipelines.
This paper provides a theoretically sound and computationally efficient solution to the critical problem of fair contribution valuation in vertical federated learning.
This paper introduces a novel and highly efficient prompt-based adversarial attack that cleverly uses the LLM's own capabilities to audit its robustness.
The paper proposes a flexible, training-free method for steganography with diffusion models that achieves impressive capacity and image quality.
This paper makes a strong theoretical contribution by solving a more general version of the matching bandit problem with provably optimal regret.
The paper rigorously investigates a critical, under-explored multilingual vulnerability in LLMs and proposes a practical, empirically validated defense mechanism.
This work presents a well-motivated and empirically validated plug-in method that provides a solid, incremental improvement to multivariate time series forecasting.
The paper proposes a technically sound and well-motivated knowledge distillation method that achieves significant, quantifiable improvements in GNN compression.
The paper introduces a rigorous, quantitative method to analyze instruction data, providing valuable scientific insight and a practical data-curation technique.
This is a solid, well-motivated architectural contribution to a classic computer vision problem, demonstrating improved performance on standard benchmarks.
The paper presents a highly novel approach that cleverly repurposes the generative capabilities of LLMs to replace an entire component of the standard retrieval-augmented pipeline.

The work provides a rigorous and efficient solution to the critical problem of fair contribution valuation in vertical federated learning, supported by both theoretical analysis and empirical results.
The paper presents a novel and efficient prompt-based methodology for generating adversarial attacks, providing a valuable tool for auditing LLM robustness.
The work proposes an elegant, training-free method for steganography that leverages diffusion models to achieve a strong, well-quantified balance of capacity, quality, and accuracy.
This paper makes a fundamental theoretical contribution to matching bandits by solving the more general two-sided problem and providing an algorithm with provably optimal regret.
The paper's central claims rest on an undefined "unsafe content" metric and lack any statistical variance reporting, making the results impossible to verify.
The method is evaluated on a reasonable number of datasets, but the reported average improvement lacks the statistical rigor needed to confirm its significance.
The paper fails to provide evidence of statistical significance for its accuracy improvements, and it is unclear if an ablation study validates the proposed attention mechanism.
The central claim is critically undermined by the absence of a random sampling baseline, making it impossible to conclude that their tagging method is responsible for the performance gain.
The approach is evaluated on standard, competitive benchmarks, but the claim of superiority is not supported by any discussion of statistical significance in the abstract.
The abstract fails to compare the proposed method against standard retrieval-augmented baselines, making it impossible to assess if the approach is practically viable.
The paper makes clear theoretical claims about fairness and efficiency and describes a comprehensive validation strategy combining both theoretical analysis and extensive experiments.
The paper compares its attack against standard baselines on relevant models, but its claims of superiority lack the necessary statistical validation.
The paper makes strong claims of outperforming prior work but provides zero quantitative comparisons in the abstract, rendering its own results contextless and its claims unsubstantiated.
The paper makes strong, optimal theoretical claims which are the core of its contribution, but it lacks any empirical validation in the abstract.
Addresses a critical and under-explored area of LLM safety with a novel dataset and a practical self-defense framework.
The proposed plug-in method for leveraging leading indicators is intuitive and demonstrates a solid performance improvement across various datasets.
This work introduces a novel attention-based knowledge distillation technique that achieves impressive GNN compression ratios with minimal accuracy degradation.
Provides a novel and highly effective framework for analyzing and improving SFT data by quantifying instruction diversity and complexity.
Introduces a well-motivated hierarchical network that explicitly models body structure, leading to improved performance on standard pose estimation benchmarks.
This work presents a highly innovative approach that unifies retrieval and generation by using the LLM itself as a retriever, potentially simplifying and improving knowledge-intensive NLP tasks.
Provides a theoretically sound and computationally efficient solution to the critical problem of fair contribution valuation in vertical federated learning.
Introduces a creative and efficient prompt-based adversarial attack that cleverly uses the LLM's own capabilities to generate examples that fool itself.
Proposes a novel and flexible training-free steganography method for diffusion models that achieves impressive capacity and image quality.
This work provides a strong theoretical contribution by extending matching bandits to the more realistic two-sided uncertainty setting and designing an algorithm with optimal regret.
The paper clearly articulates the multilingual jailbreak problem, presents quantified experimental findings, and proposes a specific, understandable solution.
The abstract effectively contrasts its approach with existing methods and clearly explains the core concept of using leading indicators for forecasting improvement.
This paper presents a clear motivation for GNN compression and explains how its proposed attention-based distillation method improves upon existing techniques.
The paper introduces a novel and intuitive method, InsTag, for analyzing SFT data, and clearly demonstrates its value through improved model performance.
The core concept of a 'parse graph' provides a clear and intuitive motivation for the proposed hierarchical network architecture for human pose estimation.
The paper proposes a novel, counter-intuitive approach to retrieval and explains the two-stage methodology with excellent clarity.
The paper clearly defines the problem of contribution valuation in vertical federated learning and presents its proposed solution, VerFedSV, by highlighting its fairness and efficiency.
The paper introduces a clever and clearly explained adversarial attack method where the LLM is prompted to fool itself, with a well-structured description of the prompt components.
The abstract exceptionally clearly explains its novel, training-free approach to steganography using diffusion models and presents compelling, quantified results on capacity and quality.
While technically dense, the paper clearly situates its contribution within the matching bandits literature and specifies the theoretical guarantees of its proposed algorithm.
The abstract clearly and concisely presents a well-defined problem, specific quantitative findings, and a concrete proposed solution.
This abstract effectively communicates a clear gap in current methods and presents a well-reasoned, intuitive solution with quantified performance gains.
The paper presents a compelling narrative, clearly identifying a limitation in prior work and introducing a novel, well-explained method with strong, specific results.
The abstract successfully introduces a novel conceptual framework and demonstrates its value through both analysis and a practical data-selection application.
While the core idea of using a body parse graph is intuitive, the abstract's description of the network architecture is dense and somewhat difficult to parse on a first read.
The abstract does an exceptional job of communicating a highly novel and intriguing approach in a clear, step-by-step manner, making its bold claims easy to understand.
The abstract clearly articulates the practical problem of fair contribution valuation, explains the limitations of existing methods, and presents its proposed solution's benefits effectively.
The paper clearly presents a novel adversarial attack method, breaking down the components of the attack prompt for easy comprehension and highlighting intriguing findings.
The abstract expertly introduces the problem domain, pinpoints the limitations of prior art, and clearly explains its novel, training-free approach with impressive, quantified results.
For its target audience, the abstract clearly delineates the extension from one-sided to a more general two-sided problem and effectively communicates its theoretical guarantees.
This paper addresses a critical and timely safety issue in LLMs with a novel multilingual perspective and proposes a practical self-defense framework.
The proposed plug-in method offers a promising and generalizable approach to improving time series forecasting by leveraging lead-lag relationships.
The paper presents a novel attention-based knowledge distillation method for GNNs that achieves impressive compression rates with a simultaneous increase in accuracy.
The paper introduces a novel and practical method for analyzing SFT data, leading to better data selection and more efficient model training.
The proposed method's use of a parse graph to model body structure appears to be a promising direction for improving human pose estimation.
This paper proposes a novel and elegant generative retrieval approach that cleverly uses the LLM itself to replace traditional retrieval models.
The paper makes a valuable contribution to federated learning by proposing an efficient and fair method for valuing contributions from different data sources.
The proposed prompt-based attack is a clever and efficient method for evaluating the adversarial robustness of large language models.
This work presents a novel and powerful training-free steganography method that effectively leverages modern diffusion models to achieve high capacity and image quality.
The paper provides a significant theoretical contribution by designing an algorithm for the two-sided matching bandit problem that achieves optimal regret.
This paper addresses the critical and under-explored issue of multilingual jailbreaks with a sound methodology and a practical, empirically validated solution.
The paper makes a novel and practical contribution by logically challenging channel-independent assumptions and proposing an effective plug-in solution.
This paper presents a principled extension to knowledge distillation for GNNs, achieving strong empirical results on a practical compression task.
This work introduces a highly novel and principled methodology for analyzing SFT data, addressing a key open question with significant practical implications.
This paper provides a solid, incremental contribution to its domain by applying well-established principles of structured, hierarchical modeling.
This work proposes a conceptually elegant and paradigm-shifting approach to retrieval by using the LLM itself, demonstrating remarkable potential.
The paper addresses the important problem of fair credit assignment in federated learning with a theoretically grounded and computationally efficient approach.
This paper introduces a clever and novel attack vector that provides a useful new tool for evaluating the adversarial robustness of LLMs.
The proposed training-free method is a novel and practical application of diffusion models to steganography, achieving impressive performance.
This paper makes a strong theoretical contribution by solving a more general and challenging version of the matching bandits problem with optimal regret.
The paper addresses a critical and under-explored ethical issue in LLM safety, proposing a valuable framework for mitigating multilingual jailbreaks.
This paper proposes a technically sound and promising plug-in to improve forecasting accuracy by capturing lead-lag relationships.
The work presents a promising method for GNN compression that could significantly reduce computational costs and improve accessibility, an important contribution to responsible AI.
The paper offers a novel and effective method for analyzing instruction data, leading to better data curation, more efficient model training, and a deeper understanding of SFT.
The paper proposes an interesting, structurally-motivated approach to human pose estimation with strong results, although it omits discussion of the dual-use nature of the technology.
This work presents a creative and potentially more efficient paradigm for retrieval by using the LLM itself, though it inherits the LLM's risks of hallucination.
The paper makes a significant contribution to fair and responsible AI by proposing an efficient and provably fair method for contribution valuation in vertical federated learning.
The paper introduces an innovative and efficient adversarial attack that serves as a valuable tool for auditing and improving the robustness of large language models.
While technically impressive, the paper fails to discuss the significant dual-use risks and potential for misuse inherent in steganography research.
The paper provides a strong theoretical contribution by solving a more general and challenging version of the two-sided matching problem with bandits.
The paper provides a clear methodology and makes its data publicly available, which is very helpful for verification and future research.
The authors helpfully provide their code, and the proposed method's design as a plugin seems to make it easier for others to reproduce and build upon.
While the proposed method appears promising, the absence of a link to code or artifacts makes it challenging to fully verify the experimental setup.
The paper introduces an interesting method for analyzing instructions, though its reproducibility would be greatly enhanced by the release of the proposed tags and models.
The proposed network architecture sounds quite interesting, but reproducing the results would likely be challenging without access to the source code.
This paper presents a very clever and novel approach, though providing the specific prompts and code would be essential for others to accurately reproduce the method.
The work appears to be well-grounded theoretically, and sharing the experimental code would greatly aid in verifying its practical performance and efficiency claims.
The paper presents an intriguing attack method and helpfully provides the source code, allowing the community to easily verify and study the proposed approach.
The proposed training-free approach is very compelling, and access to the implementation of the message projection technique would solidify its reproducibility.
This appears to be a strong theoretical contribution with clear claims, providing a good foundation for future work in this matching bandits setting.
This paper identifies a critical and timely safety vulnerability in LLMs across multiple languages and proposes a practical, data-driven framework to mitigate it.
The work introduces an intuitive and effective plugin for time series forecasting that addresses a clear gap in existing channel-independent models.
The paper proposes a well-motivated knowledge distillation technique for GNNs that intelligently uses intermediate representations, backed by strong empirical results on a large-scale dataset.
This work provides a novel and practical method for quantifying the abstract concepts of instruction diversity and complexity, leading to more efficient data curation for SFT.
The paper introduces an intuitive, human-inspired structural model for pose estimation that demonstrates strong performance on standard benchmarks.
The paper proposes a paradigm-shifting approach to retrieval by using the LLM itself as a generative retriever, impressively simplifying the traditional RAG pipeline.
This work provides a theoretically sound and computationally efficient solution to the critical problem of fair contribution assessment in vertical federated learning.
The paper presents a creative prompt-based adversarial attack where the model generates its own failures, offering a new perspective on LLM robustness evaluation.
This work cleverly leverages the latent space of modern diffusion models for a practical, training-free steganography method that achieves an impressive balance of capacity and image quality.

The paper makes a significant theoretical contribution by solving the challenging two-sided matching bandit problem with an algorithm that achieves optimal regret bounds without restrictive assumptions.
Addresses the critical and overlooked multilingual attack surface for LLM safety.
An incremental improvement on a classic time-series problem, offering marginal gains.
Provides a practical solution to the real-world problem of GNN latency and model size.
Offers a useful, quantifiable method for analyzing SFT data, an important but poorly understood area.
Applies well-known hierarchical concepts to the saturated problem of human pose estimation.
Proposes a novel retrieval-by-generation method that could simplify the dominant RAG paradigm.
A solid theoretical contribution for the niche, but important, problem of fair contribution in vertical federated learning.
Demonstrates a novel and efficient adversarial attack that cleverly turns a model's capabilities against itself.
The training-free approach to diffusion steganography makes it a broadly applicable and practical contribution.
A strong theoretical result that proves optimal regret, but its impact is limited to a specific subfield.
